source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Ethanoic acid (\ce{HC2H3O2} \quad \text{or} \quad \ce{CH3CO2H} \quad \text{or} \quad \ce{CH3COOH}
) has a much lower vapor pressure than ethanol (\ce{CH3CH2OH}). What is the most reasonable explanation?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Hydrogen bonding in ethanoic acid is the strongest attractive force and is mainly responsible for the observed data.

choice_map.A: The polarizability of two oxygen atoms increases the London forces of attraction in ethanoic acid compared with ethanol.
choice_map.B: Hydrogen bonding in ethanoic acid is the strongest attractive force and is mainly responsible for the observed data.
choice_map.C: Ethanol has an –OH group and can hydrogen bond; therefore, the London forces must cause the effect.
choice_map.D: Both ethanol and ethanoic acid have an –OH, so the difference is the dipole of the second oxygen that increases the attractive forces.

extraction_notes: 答案字段为数字索引"1"，但记录中未明确说明与选项字母或数组下标的对应关系，因此保留原值。
image 字段存在 PIL.Image 对象描述，但不是可提取的图片路径或 URL。

题目为 4 选 1 选择题，候选项已限定解释空间，降低了开放式推理难度。
不需要数值计算、图文联合推理或长链条件组合，因此多步程度偏低到中低。
